title: Sea-Level Rise Coastal Exposure And Adaptation Prioritization In Small Island Developing States
summary: Build a verified workflow for assessing where sea-level rise, coastal erosion, and storm-surge compound to create the highest exposure in Small Island Developing States, supporting adaptation prioritization without overstating the precision of local SLR projections.
why_it_matters: Small Island Developing States contribute less than 1 percent of global greenhouse emissions but face existential threats from sea-level rise. Many SIDS have maximum elevations under 5 meters, populations concentrated on coastlines, and economies dependent on climate-vulnerable tourism and fisheries. Projecting SLR at island scale is hard enough; deciding which adaptation investments to prioritize with uncertain projections is harder and more consequential.

success_metrics.target: Every SLR projection used links to a dated evidence record with model, scenario, and local downscaling method documented.
success_metrics.validation_method: Run schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against evidence.json and evidence.md.

success_metrics.target: Coastal exposure maps include uncertainty ranges for SLR, subsidence, and storm-surge components.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms that each exposure map layer includes documented uncertainty bounds.

success_metrics.target: No adaptation investment or relocation recommendation is merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status, review records, and safety flags.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review for adaptation claims, replication for all quantitative SLR and exposure analyses, and field-reality review before any adaptation prioritization.
